search:Advanced Spring + Kotlin Coroutin.content:
- Add dependencies to `build.gradle.kts`:
  ```kotlin
  implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-core")
  implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-reactor")
  ```
- Use Kotlin Coroutines for non-blocking Spring Boot applications.
- Compare reactive and coroutine approaches in building non-blocking applications.
- Set up a Spring Boot 3 project with Kotlin coroutines, PostgreSQL, and Docker.
- Expose a reactive REST API using Kotlin Flows and coroutines.
- Check out the GitHub repository: [hantsy/spring-kotlin-coroutines-sample](https://github.com/hantsy/spring-kotlin-coroutines-sample) for practical examples.

- Use `./mvnw spring-boot:run` to run the Spring Boot application.
- Ensure you have Kotlin and Coroutines dependencies in your `build.gradle.kts` or `pom.xml`.
- For reactive REST API, use `spring-boot-starter-webflux` along with Kotlin Coroutines.
- Configure `application.properties` for database connections if using Spring Data.
- Use `@EnableWebFlux` to enable reactive web support.
- Implement non-blocking services by using `suspend` functions and `CoroutineScope`.
- For testing, use `WebTestClient` from Spring WebFlux Test.
- Keep your application stateless to fully leverage the benefits of Coroutines.
- Update dependencies regularly to use the latest versions for security and performance improvements.

- **CLI Commands**:
  - `./gradlew run` to run the application.
  - `./gradlew build` to build the project.

- **Latest Versions**:
  - Kotlin Coroutines: 1.7.32
  - Spring Boot: 2.6.4

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `spring.main.task-executor.core-pool-size`: Sets the core pool size of the task executor.
  - `spring.main.task-executor.max-pool-size`: Sets the maximum pool size of the task executor.
  - `spring.main.task-executor.queue-capacity`: Sets the capacity of the queue used by the task executor.

- Use `./mvnw spring-boot:run` to run Spring Boot application.
- Ensure Spring Boot version is at least 2.4 for full coroutine support.
- Add dependencies in `build.gradle.kts`:
  ```kotlin
  implementation("org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-webflux")
  implementation("io.projectreactor.kotlin:reactor-kotlin-extensions")
  ```
- Use `@EnableWebFlux` annotation on main class to enable reactive web support.
- Utilize `@GetMapping`, `@PostMapping`, etc., with `suspend` functions for coroutine-based handlers.
- Configure application properties in `application.properties` or `application.yml`.
- For non-blocking behavior, use `Mono` and `Flux` instead of traditional `CompletableFuture` and `ListenableFuture`.
